219.name: Clamp
219.signature: @219 { val: Number, min: Number, max: Number } -> Number
219.description: Clamp value between min and max

302.name: Substring
302.signature: @302 { val: String, start: Integer, len: Integer } -> String

304.name: Replace
304.signature: @304 { val: String, old: String, new: String } -> String

310.name: StartsWith
310.description: Check if string starts with prefix

311.name: EndsWith
311.description: Check if string ends with suffix

402.name: ArrSet
402.signature: @402 { arr: Array, idx: Integer, val: Value } -> Array

407.name: ArrSlice
407.signature: @407 { arr: Array, start: Integer, end: Integer } -> Array

410.name: ArrReduce
410.signature: @410 { arr: Array, fn: Function, init: Value } -> Value

500.name: If
500.signature: @500 { cond: Bool, then: Block, else: Block }

502.name: For
502.signature: @502 { init: Statement, cond: Bool, step: Statement, body: Block }

505.name: Switch
505.signature: @505 { val: Value, cases: Object, default: Block }
505.example: @505 { val: x, cases: {1: b1, 2: b2}, default: b3 }

603.name: HttpRequest
603.signature: @603 { method: String, url: String, body: Value, headers: Object } -> Object

605.name: Snapshot
605.description: Capture full execution state (PC, Stack, Registers)

606.name: WriteSnapshot
606.description: Write a snapshot handle to a JSON file

616.name: ReadCSV
616.description: Read CSV file to array of objects

617.name: WriteCSV
617.description: Write array of objects to CSV

621.name: UdpSend
621.signature: @621 { host: String, port: Integer, data: Bytes } -> Bool

700.name: ScreenCapture
700.description: Capture a screenshot of the specified monitor

701.name: PipeWrite
701.description: Write tensor data (as raw bytes) to a subprocess pipe

702.name: PipeOpen
702.description: Open a subprocess and get a pipe handle

809.name: RegexReplace
809.signature: @809 { regex: String, text: String, replace: String } -> String

900.name: VulkanShader
900.signature: @900 { spirv_binary: bytes, entry_point: String, shader_stage: String, descriptor_bindings: [Binding] }

901.name: ComputeKernel
901.signature: @901 { kernel_name: String, shader_handle: Handle, workgroup_size: [u32; 3], shared_memory_bytes: u32, push_constants_layout: String }

902.name: PipelineConfig
902.signature: @902 { pipeline_id: String, stages: [ShaderStage], sync_barriers: [Barrier], output_image: String }

906.name: GEMM
906.example: let C = @906 { A: matrix_a, B: matrix_b };
906.usage: Matrix multiplication for neural networks, linear algebra

908.name: GELU
908.description: Gaussian Error Linear Unit activation function
908.usage: Transformer activation function (modern alternative to ReLU)

910.name: CrossEntropy
910.example: let loss = @910 { predictions: model_out, targets: labels };

915.name: BatchNorm
915.signature: @915 { x: Tensor, mean: Tensor, var: Tensor } -> Tensor
915.example: let norm = @915 { x: input, mean: m, var: v };

notes: Contract IDs are permanent and immutable once assigned
Each tier has semantic meaning - respect the tier boundaries
GPU contracts (T4) require Vulkan runtime support
Compiler-internal contracts (T1) are not directly user-accessible
Application-specific contracts (T5+) have no central registration requirement
